ce:section.ce:para: This review summarizes how Sentinel-1 synthetic aperture radar products are being used across offshore wind-energy workflows, with emphasis on high spatial detail, wake mapping and practical wind-resource interpretation.

The fixture keeps the structure of a long review article while reducing the text to a compact offline sample that still exercises section parsing, heading detection and AI-markdown serialization for a realistic Elsevier XML case.

The article compares operational Sentinel-1 wind products produced by public services and research groups, including differences in resolution, geophysical model functions and input wind-direction sources.

Wind direction can be estimated from streaks inside the SAR scene or imported from models and local measurements. The appropriate source depends on scene quality, coastal complexity and the scale of the target application.

Longer observational archives make it possible to compare wind-resource statistics across years and to ask how coverage, revisit time and auxiliary stability information influence estimates used by the offshore wind sector.
